# fngr

> Modern gesture recognition library for the web. PointerEvent-based, TypeScript-first, tree-shakeable, zero dependencies, framework-agnostic, SSR-safe.

## Install

```
npm install fngr
```

## Quick Start

Each gesture has a one-liner convenience function that returns a cleanup function:

```ts
import { tap } from 'fngr/tap';
import { doubleTap } from 'fngr/doubletap';
import { longPress } from 'fngr/longpress';
import { pan } from 'fngr/pan';
import { swipe } from 'fngr/swipe';
import { pinch } from 'fngr/pinch';
import { rotate } from 'fngr/rotate';

const off = tap(element, (e) => console.log('tapped', e.pointers[0].clientX));
off(); // cleanup
```

## Imports

Recognizers are tree-shakeable subpath exports — import only what you use:

| Gesture    | Convenience function | Class                  | Import path        |
|------------|---------------------|------------------------|--------------------|
| Tap        | `tap()`             | `TapRecognizer`        | `fngr/tap`         |
| Double Tap | `doubleTap()`       | `DoubleTapRecognizer`  | `fngr/doubletap`   |
| Long Press | `longPress()`       | `LongPressRecognizer`  | `fngr/longpress`   |
| Swipe      | `swipe()`           | `SwipeRecognizer`      | `fngr/swipe`       |
| Pan        | `pan()`             | `PanRecognizer`        | `fngr/pan`         |
| Pinch      | `pinch()`           | `PinchRecognizer`      | `fngr/pinch`       |
| Rotate     | `rotate()`          | `RotateRecognizer`     | `fngr/rotate`      |

Core: `Manager`, `BaseRecognizer`, types — import from `fngr`.

---

## Tap

Recognizes a single-finger tap (pointer down then up).

```ts
import { tap } from 'fngr/tap';

// Callback form
const off = tap(el, (e) => console.log(e.type)); // 'tap'

// Options form
const off = tap(el, {
  threshold: 10,  // max movement in px (default 10)
  interval: 250,  // max duration in ms (default 250)
  onTap(e) { console.log(e.pointers[0].clientX); },
});

off(); // remove recognizer
```

### TapEvent

```ts
interface TapEvent {
  type: 'tap';
  count: 1;
  target: Element;
  pointers: PointerInfo[];  // [{id, clientX, clientY, pageX, pageY}]
  timestamp: number;
  srcEvent: PointerEvent;
  preventDefault(): void;
}
```

---

## Double Tap

Recognizes two consecutive taps within an interval.

```ts
import { doubleTap } from 'fngr/doubletap';

const off = doubleTap(el, (e) => console.log('double-tapped'));

// Options form
const off = doubleTap(el, {
  threshold: 10,  // max distance between taps in px (default 10)
  interval: 300,  // max time between taps in ms (default 300)
  onDoubletap(e) { console.log(e.count); }, // always 2
});
```

### DoubleTapEvent

```ts
interface DoubleTapEvent {
  type: 'doubletap';
  count: 2;
  target: Element;
  pointers: PointerInfo[];
  timestamp: number;
  srcEvent: PointerEvent;
  preventDefault(): void;
}
```

---

## Long Press

Recognizes a press-and-hold gesture. Fires `longpress` when the hold duration is reached, and `longpressup` when the pointer lifts afterward.

```ts
import { longPress } from 'fngr/longpress';

const off = longPress(el, (e) => console.log('held for', e.duration, 'ms'));

// Options form — also listen for release
const off = longPress(el, {
  threshold: 10,  // max movement in px (default 10)
  duration: 500,  // hold time in ms (default 500)
  onLongpress(e) { console.log('longpress', e.duration); },
  onLongpressup(e) { console.log('released', e.duration); },
});
```

### LongPressEvent

```ts
interface LongPressEvent {
  type: 'longpress' | 'longpressup';
  duration: number;   // ms from pointer-down to event firing
  target: Element;
  pointers: PointerInfo[];
  timestamp: number;
  srcEvent: PointerEvent;
  preventDefault(): void;
}
```

---

## Swipe

Recognizes a single-finger directional flick. Checks distance, velocity, and optional direction filter on pointer-up.

```ts
import { swipe } from 'fngr/swipe';

const off = swipe(el, (e) => console.log('swiped', e.direction));

// Options form — restrict to horizontal, custom thresholds
const off = swipe(el, {
  threshold: 50,   // min distance in px (default 30)
  velocity: 0.5,   // min speed in px/ms (default 0.3)
  direction: 'horizontal',  // 'all' | 'horizontal' | 'vertical' (default 'all')
  onSwipe(e) { console.log(e.direction, e.distance, e.velocity); },
});
```

### SwipeEvent

```ts
interface SwipeEvent {
  type: 'swipe';
  direction: 'left' | 'right' | 'up' | 'down';
  distance: number;   // straight-line distance in px
  velocity: number;   // speed in px/ms
  target: Element;
  pointers: PointerInfo[];
  timestamp: number;
  srcEvent: PointerEvent;
  preventDefault(): void;
}
```

Direction is determined by dominant axis: `|dx| > |dy|` → horizontal, otherwise vertical.

---

## Pan

Recognizes a single-finger drag (pan) gesture. Continuous lifecycle: panstart → panmove (repeating) → panend | pancancel.

```ts
import { pan } from 'fngr/pan';

const off = pan(el, (e) => console.log('pan started', e.deltaX));

// Options form — restrict to horizontal, custom threshold
const off = pan(el, {
  threshold: 10,     // min distance in px before recognition (default 10)
  direction: 'horizontal',  // 'all' | 'horizontal' | 'vertical' (default 'all')
  onPanstart(e) { console.log('start', e.deltaX, e.deltaY); },
  onPanmove(e) { console.log('move', e.deltaX, e.deltaY, e.direction); },
  onPanend(e) { console.log('end', e.deltaX, e.deltaY); },
  onPancancel(e) { console.log('cancelled'); },
});
```

### PanEvent

```ts
interface PanEvent {
  type: 'panstart' | 'panmove' | 'panend' | 'pancancel';
  deltaX: number;     // displacement from pointer-down in px
  deltaY: number;
  velocityX: number;  // px/ms
  velocityY: number;
  direction: Direction;  // 'left' | 'right' | 'up' | 'down' | 'none'
  isFirst: boolean;   // true only on panstart
  isFinal: boolean;   // true only on panend/pancancel
  target: Element;
  pointers: PointerInfo[];
  timestamp: number;
  srcEvent: PointerEvent;
  preventDefault(): void;
}
```

---

## Pinch

Recognizes a two-finger pinch (zoom) gesture. Continuous lifecycle: pinchstart → pinchmove (repeating) → pinchend | pinchcancel.

```ts
import { pinch } from 'fngr/pinch';

const off = pinch(el, {
  threshold: 0,     // min scale change before recognition (default 0)
  onPinchstart(e) { console.log('start', e.scale); },
  onPinchmove(e) { console.log('move', e.scale, e.deltaScale); },
  onPinchend(e) { console.log('end', e.scale); },
  onPinchcancel(e) { console.log('cancelled'); },
});
```

### PinchEvent

```ts
interface PinchEvent {
  type: 'pinchstart' | 'pinchmove' | 'pinchend' | 'pinchcancel';
  scale: number;       // cumulative scale factor (1 = no change)
  deltaScale: number;  // change since last event
  center: Point;       // midpoint between the two pointers {x, y}
  isFirst: boolean;    // true only on pinchstart
  isFinal: boolean;    // true only on pinchend/pinchcancel
  target: Element;
  pointers: PointerInfo[];
  timestamp: number;
  srcEvent: PointerEvent;
  preventDefault(): void;
}
```

Scale = currentDistance / initialDistance. `scale > 1` = zoom in, `scale < 1` = zoom out.

State machine: Idle → (2x pointerdown) → Possible → (scale change > threshold) → Began → Changed → Ended | Cancelled.

---

## Rotate

Recognizes a two-finger rotation gesture. Continuous lifecycle: rotatestart → rotatemove (repeating) → rotateend | rotatecancel.

```ts
import { rotate } from 'fngr/rotate';

const off = rotate(el, {
  threshold: 0,     // min rotation in degrees before recognition (default 0)
  onRotatestart(e) { console.log('start', e.rotation); },
  onRotatemove(e) { console.log('move', e.rotation, e.deltaRotation); },
  onRotateend(e) { console.log('end', e.rotation); },
  onRotatecancel(e) { console.log('cancelled'); },
});
```

### RotateEvent

```ts
interface RotateEvent {
  type: 'rotatestart' | 'rotatemove' | 'rotateend' | 'rotatecancel';
  rotation: number;       // cumulative rotation in degrees (positive = clockwise)
  deltaRotation: number;  // change since last event
  center: Point;          // midpoint between the two pointers {x, y}
  isFirst: boolean;       // true only on rotatestart
  isFinal: boolean;       // true only on rotateend/rotatecancel
  target: Element;
  pointers: PointerInfo[];
  timestamp: number;
  srcEvent: PointerEvent;
  preventDefault(): void;
}
```

Rotation = normalizeAngle(currentAngle - initialAngle). Positive = clockwise, negative = counter-clockwise.

State machine: Idle → (2x pointerdown) → Possible → (rotation change > threshold) → Began → Changed → Ended | Cancelled.

---

## Manager + Class API

For composing multiple recognizers on one element, or for arbitration:

```ts
import { Manager } from 'fngr';
import { TapRecognizer } from 'fngr/tap';
import { DoubleTapRecognizer } from 'fngr/doubletap';

const manager = new Manager(el);

const dt = new DoubleTapRecognizer({
  interval: 300,
  onDoubletap(e) { console.log('double-tap'); },
});

const t = new TapRecognizer({
  onTap(e) { console.log('single tap'); },
});

// Tap waits for double-tap to fail before firing
t.requireFailureOf(dt);

// Higher priority gets pointer events first
manager.add(dt, { priority: 10 });
manager.add(t);

// Cleanup
manager.destroy();
```

### Manager methods

- `new Manager(element)` — attaches pointer listeners, sets `touch-action: none`
- `manager.add(recognizer, { priority? })` — register recognizer
- `manager.remove(recognizer)` — unregister
- `manager.destroy()` — remove all listeners, destroy recognizers, restore `touch-action`

### Arbitration

- `recognizer.requireFailureOf(other)` — defer recognition until `other` fails
- `recognizer.allowSimultaneous(other)` — let both recognize at the same time

---

## CustomEvents

Every gesture also dispatches a DOM `CustomEvent` on the target element, so you can use standard `addEventListener`:

```ts
el.addEventListener('fngr:tap', (e) => {
  const detail = (e as CustomEvent).detail; // TapEvent
});

el.addEventListener('fngr:doubletap', (e) => { /* DoubleTapEvent */ });
el.addEventListener('fngr:longpress', (e) => { /* LongPressEvent */ });
el.addEventListener('fngr:longpressup', (e) => { /* LongPressEvent */ });
el.addEventListener('fngr:swipe', (e) => { /* SwipeEvent */ });
el.addEventListener('fngr:panstart', (e) => { /* PanEvent */ });
el.addEventListener('fngr:panmove', (e) => { /* PanEvent */ });
el.addEventListener('fngr:panend', (e) => { /* PanEvent */ });
el.addEventListener('fngr:pancancel', (e) => { /* PanEvent */ });
el.addEventListener('fngr:pinchstart', (e) => { /* PinchEvent */ });
el.addEventListener('fngr:pinchmove', (e) => { /* PinchEvent */ });
el.addEventListener('fngr:pinchend', (e) => { /* PinchEvent */ });
el.addEventListener('fngr:pinchcancel', (e) => { /* PinchEvent */ });
el.addEventListener('fngr:rotatestart', (e) => { /* RotateEvent */ });
el.addEventListener('fngr:rotatemove', (e) => { /* RotateEvent */ });
el.addEventListener('fngr:rotateend', (e) => { /* RotateEvent */ });
el.addEventListener('fngr:rotatecancel', (e) => { /* RotateEvent */ });
```

Events bubble and are cancelable.

---

## PointerInfo

All gesture events include a `pointers` array:

```ts
interface PointerInfo {
  id: number;       // PointerEvent.pointerId
  clientX: number;  // relative to viewport
  clientY: number;
  pageX: number;    // relative to document
  pageY: number;
}
```

---

## State Machine

Each recognizer is a state machine:

Discrete gestures (tap, doubletap, longpress): `Idle → Possible → Recognized | Failed`
Continuous gestures (pan, pinch, rotate): `Idle → Possible → Began → Changed → Ended | Cancelled`

After terminal states (`Recognized`, `Failed`, `Ended`, `Cancelled`), recognizers auto-reset to `Idle`.

---

## Design Constraints

- PointerEvent only — no Touch/MouseEvent fallbacks
- ESM only — `.mjs` output with TypeScript declarations
- SSR-safe — no top-level DOM access
- Zero runtime dependencies
- Tree-shakeable — each recognizer is an independent entry point
